meta.subject: Social Studies
meta.source: NCDC Primary Six Curriculum Set One, Social Studies Syllabus, Theme: People and Resources of East Africa
meta.notes: P6 Social Studies strictly focuses on the East African region (Uganda, Kenya, Tanzania, Rwanda, Burundi, South Sudan).

themes.name: People and Resources of East Africa

topics.title: Location of East Africa on the Map of Africa
subTopics: Position and size of East Africa using latitudes and longitudes
Neighboring countries and bodies of water surrounding East Africa
Member states of the East African Community (EAC) and their capital cities
Landlocked and coastal countries of East Africa

topics.title: Physical Features of East Africa
subTopics: Major physical features: East African Rift Valley, block mountains (Rwenzori) and volcanic peaks (Kilimanjaro, Elgon, Kenya)
Lakes and rivers of East Africa (Lake Victoria, Tanganyika, River Nile, Tana, Rufiji)
Formation of the Great Rift Valley and basin lakes
Importance of physical features to the economy and settlement of East Africa

topics.title: Climate and Natural Vegetation of East Africa
subTopics: Climatic zones of East Africa: Equatorial, Tropical/Savanna, Semi-arid, and Montane zones
Factors influencing East African climate (altitude, relief, winds, distance from sea)
Vegetation belts of East Africa (rainforests, savanna grasslands, mountain forests, papyrus/mangrove)
Conservation of East African forests and wildlife in game parks

topics.title: The People and Pre-Colonial History of East Africa
subTopics: Origins and migration routes of Bantu, Nilotes (River-Lake, Plain, Highland), and Cushites into East Africa
Settlement patterns and traditional governance (kingdoms and chiefdoms e.g., Buganda, Bunyoro, Nyamwezi)
Long-distance trade in pre-colonial East Africa (Akamba, Nyamwezi, Arab/Swahili coastal civilization)

topics.title: Foreign Influence and Colonization of East Africa
subTopics: Scramble and partition of East Africa (Anglo-German agreements 1886/1890)
Chartered companies (Imperial British East Africa Company IBEAC and German East Africa Company)
Colonial administration: British protectorate rule in Uganda/Kenya vs German direct rule in Tanganyika
African resistance and collaboration in East Africa (Chief Mkwawa, Kabalega, Mwanga, Koitalel)

topics.title: Road to Independence and the East African Community
subTopics: Rise of nationalist movements in East Africa (TANU in Tanzania, KANU in Kenya, UNC/UPC in Uganda)
Key East African nationalists (Julius Nyerere, Jomo Kenyatta, Milton Obote, Ignatius Musaazi)
The East African Community (EAC): formation, headquarters in Arusha, organs, benefits, and challenges of regional integration
